mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding output in manufacturing, which leads to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when INDPRO above its 12-month moving average are not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) over a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) to levels below the natural rate signals labor market tightness that eventually forces the Fed to tighten policy, reducing forward stock returns.
failure_condition: If after UNRATE crosses below 4.5%, the subsequent 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3% year-over-year and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below 0.5%, negative real rates push investors out of cash into equities, boosting forward returns.
regime_hint: post-pandemic recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If the interaction signal produces Rank IC below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window, or if positive real rates occur and returns do not turn negative.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising and above 2%), high retail sales (RSAFS) signal excessive demand that may cause the Fed to tighten further, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: If in periods when FEDFUNDS > 2% and RSAFS growth > 5% YoY, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A strong monthly payrolls number (PAYEMS) above the pre-pandemic peak of 152,000 thousand signals a robust labor market, boosting consumer confidence and equity prices in the short term.
failure_condition: If when PAYEMS exceeds 152,000, the 5-day forward return is positive less than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts (HOUST) and industrial production (INDPRO) above their respective 12-month moving averages indicate a broad-based expansion that supports higher stock prices.
failure_condition: If the joint condition does not lead to positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP (GDPC1) expands the corporate earnings base and supports higher stock prices over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is less than 0.2 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-COVID recovery characterized by zero interest rates (FEDFUNDS near zero), high retail sales (RSAFS) reflect strong demand that boosts corporate profits and yields positive forward returns.
regime_hint: post-pandemic recovery with zero interest rates
failure_condition: If in periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, RSAFS above 600,000 million leads to positive 20-day returns less than 70% of the time.
